children.label: a
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary concerned, or his designated representative, leave may be taken by a member on a calendar-day basis as vacation or absence from duty with pay, annually as accruing, or otherwise.
provide equal treatment of officers and enlisted members;
establish to the fullest extent practicable uniform policies for the several armed forces;
provide that leave shall be taken annually as accruing to the extent consistent with military requirements and other exigencies; and

provide for the determination of the number of calendar days of leave to which a member is entitled, including the number of calendar days of absence from duty or vacation to be counted or charged against leave.

children.heading: .—Facilitating Granting of Leave for Attendance at Hearings
children.label: c .—Facilitating Granting of Leave for Attendance at Hearings
A
children.text: the leave is needed for the member to attend a hearing described in paragraph (2);

children.label: B
children.text: the member is not serving in or with a unit deployed in a contingency operation; and

children.label: C
children.text: the exigencies of military service (as determined by the Secretary concerned) do not otherwise require that such leave not be granted.

children.label: A
children.text: to determine whether a member of the armed forces is a natural parent of a child; or

children.label: B
children.text: to determine an obligation of a member of the armed forces to provide child support.

children.label: A
children.text: The term “court” has the meaning given that term in .section 1408(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: The term “child support” has the meaning given that term in section 459(i) of the Social Security Act ().42 U.S.C. 659(i)
